revision.text:
{{Nihongo|'''Shoku Nihon Kōki'''|続日本後紀||}} is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 869, it is the fourth volume in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]. It covers the years 833-850.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Kōki]]'' (840), in 855 [[Emperor Saga]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. Primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]] and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitsuna]], the text was completed in 869.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within 20 volumes, the contents covered 18 years spanning 833 and 850. As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of [[Emperor Ninmyō]] setting the model for future national histories.
==References==
* {{cite book | last = Kubota | first = Jun | title = Iwanami Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | date = 2007 | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-00-080310-6 }} * {{cite book | last = Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten Henshū Iinkai | title = Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | date = 1986 | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 4-00-080067-1 }}
{{Japan-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shoku Nihon Koki}}
[[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:History books]]
[[ja:続日本後紀]]

{{Nihongo|'''Shoku Nihon Kōki'''|続日本後紀||}} is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 869, it is the fourth volume in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]. It covers the years 833-850.

Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within 20 volumes, the contents covered 18 years spanning 833 and 850. As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of [[Emperor Ninmyō]] setting the model for future national histories.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable resource in recreating lost contents of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''
==References==

{{Japan-stub}}

{{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Shoku Nihon Kōki'''''|続日本後紀||}} is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 869, it is the fourth volume in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]. It covers the years 833–850.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Kōki]]'' (840), in 855 [[Emperor Montoku]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. Primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]] and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitsuna]], the text was completed in 869.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within 20 volumes, the contents covered 18 years spanning 833 and 850. As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of [[Emperor Ninmyō]] setting the model for future national histories.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable resource in recreating lost contents of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}

{{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Shoku Nihon Kōki'''''|続日本後紀||}} is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 869, it is the fourth volume in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]. It covers the years 833–850.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Kōki]]'' (840), in 855 [[Emperor Montoku]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. Primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]] and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitsuna]], the text was completed in 869.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within 20 volumes, the contents covered 18 years spanning 833 and 850. As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of [[Emperor Ninmyō]] setting the model for future national histories.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable resource in recreating lost contents of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/shokukouki.html Text of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''] (Japanese) *Manuscript scans, [[Waseda University Library]]: manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_08539/ 1], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0036/ 2]

{{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Shoku Nihon Kōki'''''|続日本後紀||}} is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 869, it is the fourth volume in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]. It covers the years 833–850.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Kōki]]'' (840), in 855 [[Emperor Montoku]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. Primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]] and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitsuna]], the text was completed in 869.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within 20 volumes, the contents covered 18 years spanning 833 and 850. As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of [[Emperor Ninmyō]] setting the model for future national histories.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable resource in recreating lost contents of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/shokukouki.html Text of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''] (Japanese) *Manuscript scans, [[Waseda University Library]]: manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_08539/ 1], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0036/ 2]

{{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Shoku Nihon Kōki'''''|続日本後紀||}} is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 869, it is the fourth volume in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]. It covers the years 833–850.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Kōki]]'' (840), in 855 [[Emperor Montoku]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. Primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]] and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitsuna]], the text was completed in 869.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within 20 volumes, the contents covered 18 years spanning 833 and 850. As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of [[Emperor Ninmyō]] setting the model for future national histories.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable resource in recreating lost contents of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''

Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Kōki]]'' (840), in 855 [[Emperor Montoku]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. Primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]] and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitsuna]], the text was completed in 869.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within 20 volumes, the contents covered 18 years spanning 833 and 850. As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of [[Emperor Ninmyō]] setting the model for future national histories.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable resource in recreating lost contents of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}

As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]] setting the model for future national histories.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable resource in recreating lost contents of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[cs:Šoku Nihonkóki]][[de:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[en:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[es:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[ff:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[fr:Shoku nihon kōki]][[ja:続日本後紀]][[ko:속일본후기]][[pt:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[zh:續日本後紀]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[cs:Šoku Nihonkóki]][[de:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[en:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[es:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[ff:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[fr:Shoku nihon kōki]][[ja:続日本後紀]][[ko:속일본후기]][[pt:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[zh:續日本後紀]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[cs:Šoku Nihonkóki]][[de:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[en:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[es:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[ff:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[fr:Shoku nihon kōki]][[ja:続日本後紀]][[ko:속일본후기]][[pt:Shoku Nihon Kōki]][[zh:續日本後紀]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1041301]] Wikidata name: Shoku Nihon Kōki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Shoku Nihon Kōki'''''|続日本後紀||}} is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 869, it is the fourth volume in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]. It covers the years 833–850.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]'' (840), in 855 [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. Primarily edited by [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]] and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitsuna]], the text was completed in 869.
==Contents==
Written in [[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]-style and contained within 20 volumes, the contents covered 18 years spanning 833 and 850. As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]] setting the model for future national histories.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable resource in recreating lost contents of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}

Completed in 869, it is the fourth volume in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]. It covers the years 833–850.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''{{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki}}'' (840), in 855 {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku}} ordered the compilation of the years since then. Primarily edited by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|en|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|WD=Q960841|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshifusa}} and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitsuna]], the text was completed in 869.
==Contents==
Written in {{ill|Kanbun|en|Kanbun|WD=Q7901319|lt=Kanbun}}-style and contained within 20 volumes, the contents covered 18 years spanning 833 and 850. As opposed to the previous national histories, it is the first to cover a single reign, that of {{ill|Emperor Ninmyō|en|Emperor Ninmyō|WD=Q349271|lt=Emperor Ninmyō}} setting the model for future national histories.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable resource in recreating lost contents of the ''Shoku Nihon Kōki''
